title: 10 best herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, used to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and improve mobility in individuals with this condition. Commonly used herbs include turmeric, willow bark, and Jamaican dogwood. These natural remedies work by reducing inflammation in the shoulder joint, easing pain, and promoting blood flow, which helps to improve flexibility and range of motion. By incorporating herbal tinctures into their treatment plan, many people with frozen shoulder have experienced significant reductions in pain and increased mobility, allowing them to return to their daily activities and enjoy an improved quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The active compounds in cannabis, such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. This can help to improve mobility and reduce stiffness in the shoulder joint, making it easier to perform daily activities. Additionally, cannabis tincture can be taken sublingually, allowing for quick and effective absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief for those suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tincture can reduce inflammation in the shoulder, allowing for increased mobility and reduced pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation.

Analgesic: Cannabis tincture can alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved range of motion and increased functionality. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate pain perception.

Muscle relaxant: Cannabis tincture can help relax the muscles surrounding the shoulder, reducing tension and improving mobility. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate muscle tension and spasticity.

Antispasmodic: Cannabis tincture can reduce muscle spasms and cramping in the shoulder, allowing for increased mobility and reduced pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate muscle contractility and reduce spasms.

Neuroprotective: Cannabis tincture can protect nerves and promote nerve healing, which can help reduce pain and improve mobility in the shoulder. The active constituents responsible for this property are cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate neuroinflammation and promote nerve health.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 ounce of cannabis flower, preferably a high-CBD strain, into a fine consistency.
Combine the ground cannabis with 2 cups of food-grade vegetable glycerin in a slow cooker, setting it to low heat.
Allow the mixture to steep for 24-48 hours, stirring occasionally to ensure even heating.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Administer 1-2 mL of the tincture, up to three times a day, by placing it under the tongue and holding for 30-60 seconds before swallowing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the cannabis plant contain high levels of cannabinoids, such as CBD, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Leaves: The leaves of the cannabis plant contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including cannabinoids and terpenes, that can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for those suffering from frozen shoulder.

Seeds: Hemp seeds, which come from the cannabis plant, are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and can help reduce inflammation in the body, making them a useful ingredient in medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder.

Stems: While not as potent as the flowers or leaves, the stems of the cannabis plant still contain beneficial compounds that can be extracted to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder, providing a more cost-effective option.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected shoulder joint. The herbal extract, rich in capsaicin, increases blood flow, promoting healing and improving mobility. Regular use of bell pepper tincture can help restore range of motion, reduce stiffness, and strengthen the shoulder muscles, providing long-term relief from the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of antioxidants like vitamin C and bioflavonoids, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, providing pain relief and improved mobility.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of bell pepper tincture, attributed to capsaicin, can alleviate pain in frozen shoulder by desensitizing pain receptors and stimulating the release of endorphins.

Circulation enhancement: Bell pepper tincture's ability to improve blood circulation, owing to the presence of vitamin B6 and niacin, can promote healing and nutrient delivery to the affected area, facilitating shoulder joint recovery.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of bell pepper tincture, resulting from the presence of magnesium and potassium, can help relax muscles and reduce spasms and tension in the shoulder, increasing range of motion.

Immune support: Bell pepper tincture's immune-boosting qualities, owing to its high vitamin C content, can help the body fight off infections and inflammation, promoting overall shoulder health and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_recipe: Chop two bell peppers and one cup of herbs known to help with inflammation, such as turmeric and ginger, then combine them in a clean jar.
Fill the jar with 80-proof vodka until the ingredients are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solid remains, and store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle.
Take 1-2 mL of the tincture up to three times a day for relief of frozen shoulder symptoms, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Stems: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Rich in essential oils and analgesic compounds, leaves are used to alleviate pain and promote blood flow to the affected area.
Flowers: Packed with vitamins and minerals, flowers have anti-spasmodic properties, providing relief from muscle tension and stiffness in frozen shoulder.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and phytonutrients, seeds are used to improve joint mobility and reduce inflammation in individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties help reduce the inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness. Additionally, curcumin inhibits the activity of enzymes and proteins that cause joint damage, helping to restore mobility and function to the affected shoulder. Furthermore, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the shoulder joint from further damage, promoting healing and recovery. Overall, turmeric tincture is a natural and effective remedy for managing the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of curcuminoids like curcumin, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, providing relief from pain and stiffness.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic effects of turmeric, resulting from its curcumin content, help alleviate pain and improve mobility in individuals with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of turmeric, primarily from curcumin, helps protect cells from oxidative stress and supports tissue repair in the shoulder joint.
Improves circulation: Turmeric tincture can help enhance blood flow and reduce blood platelet aggregation, allowing for better nutrient delivery and waste removal in the affected area.

Immune-modulating: Turmeric's immune-modulating properties, attributed to curcumin, can help regulate the immune response, reducing the likelihood of chronic inflammation and promoting healing in frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root and place it in a clean jar, adding 2 cups of vodka or rum to cover the herb.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
After the recommended time, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle.
Discard the solid remains and store the tincture in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Take 1-2 mL of the turmeric tincture three times a day for the treatment of frozen shoulder, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain curcumin, which has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing the stiffness and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcumin, which has been shown to improve joint mobility and reduce inflammation in individuals with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds have anti-inflammatory properties, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, which is often a major factor in the development and persistence of frozen shoulder. Additionally, the tincture may help to stimulate the production of synovial fluid, which can improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness. The herb's analgesic effects may also help to alleviate the pain associated with frozen shoulder. Overall, eastern purple coneflower tincture can help to promote healing and restore range of motion in the shoulder joint, making it an effective natural remedy for this common and often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, which is important for frozen shoulder as it alleviates pain and stiffness, and this property is attributed to the presence of choline, phenolic acids, and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The tincture has pain-relieving properties, which are crucial for managing the pain associated with frozen shoulder, due to the presence of alkamides.

Muscle relaxant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps relax muscles, which is important for frozen shoulder as it reduces tension and improves mobility, thanks to the active constituents like caffeic acid and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the shoulder joint from oxidative stress and promote healing, which is important for frozen shoulder as it supports tissue repair and reduces inflammation, due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkamides.

Circulation-enhancing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture improves blood flow to the shoulder joint, which is important for frozen shoulder as it promotes healing and reduces stiffness, due to the presence of essential oils and alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, rinse, and chop coarsely.
Combine 1 cup of chopped herb with 2 cups of 100-proof vodka in a clean jar, ensuring herb is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year, using 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) three times a day for frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help reduce inflammation in frozen shoulder.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, the leaves aid in soothing muscle tension and promoting healing.
Buds: Packed with therapeutic properties, the buds can help alleviate pain and improve mobility in frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, the seeds support joint health and lubrication, benefiting those with frozen shoulder.
Rhizomes: Acting as a natural analgesic, the rhizomes can help manage pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected shoulder joint. The herb's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, work together to increase blood flow and improve joint mobility, which can help restore range of motion and reduce stiffness. Additionally, the tincture's soothing effects can help relax muscles and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder, providing pain relief. The active constituents responsible for this property include eucalyptol and tannins.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of Tasmanian blue gum tincture help alleviate pain by numbing the affected area, promoting mobility and reducing discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of Tasmanian blue gum tincture help relax muscles and reduce spasms, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced stiffness. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and eugenol.

Circulatory stimulant: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's circulatory stimulant properties help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. The active constituents responsible for this property include methyl eugenol and alpha-terpineol.

Immunostimulant: The immunostimulant properties of Tasmanian blue gum tincture help boost the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off infection and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include limonene and globulol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from a Tasmanian blue gum tree, selecting only the young, bright green ones for optimal potency.
Chop or grind 2 cups of leaves and add to a clean glass jar, filling it up to 3/4, then pouring 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the herbs.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days to ensure even extraction.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, amber glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place or refrigerator, where it can last for up to a year. For best results, take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) of the tincture 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare practitioner.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum plant is used to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder because of their pain-relieving and healing effects.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used in the production of medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder due to their rich antioxidant content.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder because of their potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties work to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move and reducing stiffness. Additionally, devil's claw has been shown to improve joint mobility and function, making it an effective natural remedy for treating frozen shoulder. The herb's active compounds, such as harpagoside, work to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, providing relief from the symptoms of frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and relieving pain, devil's claw tincture can help restore range of motion and improve overall shoulder health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improved mobility. This is mainly due to the presence of harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Analgesic: Its analgesic effects help alleviate pain caused by frozen shoulder, enabling better sleep and rehabilitation. The main active constituents responsible for this property are harpagoside, β-sitosterol, and essential oils.
Improves mobility: Devil's claw tincture can help improve shoulder mobility by reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties.

Muscle relaxant: Devil's claw tincture has muscle relaxant properties that help relieve muscle tension around the shoulder joint, reducing pain and improving overall function. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are harpagoside, procumbide, and other iridoid glycosides.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of devil's claw tincture help protect shoulder tissues from oxidative stress and support overall joint health. The main active constituents contributing to this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and proanthocyanidins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried devil's claw root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the finished tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to 2 years. Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) 3 times daily for pain relief from frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant contain high levels of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the devil's claw plant are rich in harpagoside, a compound known for its ability to alleviate pain and inflammation in the joints, making it an ideal part for medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder.
Flowers: The flowers of the devil's claw plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing the swelling and pain in the shoulder joint.
Seeds: The seeds of the devil's claw plant contain essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help improve mobility and reduce pain in individuals with frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, work together to promote circulation and relax muscles, thereby improving mobility and reducing stiffness in the affected area. By increasing the production of serotonin and other neurotransmitters in the brain, St. John's wort tincture can also help to alleviate the emotional stress and anxiety that often accompany chronic pain conditions like frozen shoulder. Overall, St. John's wort tincture offers a natural and holistic approach to managing the symptoms of frozen shoulder, helping to restore function and mobility to the affected joint.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation in the shoulder, providing pain relief and improving mobility, due to the active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain associated with frozen shoulder, enabling patients to perform physical therapy exercises more comfortably, thanks to the presence of hypericin and other volatile oils.

Nervine: St. John's wort tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce tension and muscle spasms in the shoulder, attributed to active components such as hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Antispasmodic: The herb helps relax muscle spasms and tension in the shoulder, enhancing range of motion and reducing pain, owing to its antispasmodic properties derived from active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.

Circulation-enhancing: St. John's wort tincture improves blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness, due to the active constituents like hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar approximately 3/4 full.
Submerge the herbs completely with a menstruum of 50% vodka and 50% distilled water, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Cap the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the marc (plant material) and discarding it.
Store the finished St. John's Wort tincture in a glass dropper bottle, and take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) up to three times daily for frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, St. John's Wort flowers are used to make medicinal tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Leaves: High in hypericin and flavonoids, St. John's Wort leaves are used in tinctures for their pain-relieving, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for treating frozen shoulder.

Buds: Containing high levels of active compounds, St. John's Wort buds are used in tinctures for their potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in frozen shoulder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint. Its active compounds, such as helenalin and alantolactone, work together to improve blood circulation and promote tissue repair. By increasing the range of motion and reducing stiffness, elecampane tincture can help restore normal shoulder function and provide relief from the symptoms of frozen shoulder. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles surrounding the shoulder joint, providing further relief and promoting overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elecampane tincture helps reduce inflammation in the shoulder joint, which is important for frozen shoulder as it can alleviate pain and stiffness caused by inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Analgesic: Elecampane tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is crucial for frozen shoulder as it can help manage the often severe pain associated with the condition. The analgesic effects are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils, such as azulene.

Antispasmodic: Elecampane tincture helps relax muscles and reduce spasms, which is important for frozen shoulder as it can alleviate tension in the shoulder muscles and improve mobility. The antispasmodic properties are due to the presence of alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Circulation-enhancing: Elecampane tincture improves blood flow and circulation, which is important for frozen shoulder as it can help reduce stiffness and promote healing in the shoulder joint. Immune-boosting: Elecampane tincture supports the immune system, which is important for frozen shoulder as it can help the body fight off infections and promote healing. The immune-boosting properties are due to the presence of polysaccharides, such as inulin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried elecampane root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the finished elecampane tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle at room temperature, away from direct sunlight.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elecampane roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for frozen shoulder due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The active compounds in peppermint, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, work together to increase blood flow and relax muscles, which can help improve mobility and reduce stiffness in the shoulder. Additionally, peppermint tincture can help relieve muscle spasms and tension, providing further relief to individuals suffering from frozen shoulder. Its natural properties make it a safe and effective alternative to traditional pain relief medications.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Peppermint tincture can help alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder by activating cold-sensitive receptors, reducing the perception of pain. This property is due to the presence of menthol, a natural analgesic.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the shoulder joint, promoting mobility and reducing discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Muscle relaxant: Peppermint tincture can help relax muscles, reducing tension in the shoulder and promoting range of motion. The compound responsible for this property is menthol, which acts on muscle receptors to reduce tension.

Circulation-enhancing: Peppermint tincture can increase blood flow, bringing oxygen and nutrients to the affected area and promoting healing. This is due to the presence of menthol, which dilates blood vessels and improves circulation.

Antispasmodic: Peppermint tincture can help relieve muscle spasms and cramping, reducing pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. This property is due to the presence of menthol, which acts on muscle receptors to reduce spasms and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, filling a clean jar twice with tightly packed herb, then fill the jar with 80-proof vodka until the herb is completely submerged.
Label the jar with the plant name, menstruum (vodka), start date, and expected end date (typically 4-6 weeks).
Store the jar in a cool, dark place, shaking it daily to ensure even extraction.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the herbs from the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the finished tincture in a glass dropper bottle and use 2-3 times a day, 10-30 drops per dose, for up to 3 weeks to help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Seeds: High in antioxidants and volatile oils, peppermint seeds are used in tinctures to support overall joint health and reduce stiffness in frozen shoulder, enhancing mobility.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic herbs. For instance, a tincture made from a blend of turmeric, willow bark, and Jamaican dogwood can help reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain. These herbs have been used traditionally for treating joint pain and inflammation. It is important to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment. Regular gentle exercise and physical therapy can also aid in recovery and should be used in conjunction with the tincture.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal tinctures include adhesive capsulitis and rotator cuff injuries. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from willow bark, turmeric, and arnica, can help reduce inflammation and manage pain associated with these conditions. Willow bark contains salicin, a natural pain-reliever, while turmeric has curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Arnica, on the other hand, helps improve mobility and reduce swelling. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
